data.title: Clear Street Group postponed its IPO due to market conditions, with software sell-off spooking investors.
data.description: Clear Street Group has decided to delay its initial public offering due to current market conditions. The fintech company made this decision as a response to a recent software sell-off that has caused concern among investors. The postponement of the IPO is a strategic move by Clear Street Group to navigate the uncertain market landscape. This news highlights the impact of market volatility on companies seeking to go public. The exact numbers related to the IPO delay were not provided in the reported information.

data.title: 130,000 jobs added, 172,000 private-sector jobs; unemployment rate at 4.3%. S&P 500 fluctuates.
data.description: Employment figures have shown an increase of 130,000 payrolls, including 172,000 jobs in the private sector. The unemployment rate has dropped to 4.3%. However, some analysts believe that the strong hiring numbers may not accurately reflect the current job market situation. As a result, the S&P 500 index has been fluctuating in response to this news.

data.title: January's jobs report: U.S. added 130,000 jobs, unemployment rate at 4.3%, wage growth up 3.7% y-o-y. However, 898,000 jobs erased from 2024-2025 estimates, average monthly job creation in 2025 at 15,000 - weakest since 2003. Economists debate if January reflects stabilization or masking a weaker trend. Former Fed economist Claudia Sahm notes "huge" downward revisions.
data.description: The U.S. economy added 130,000 jobs in January, surpassing economists' expectations of 70,000, and the unemployment rate dropped from 4.4% to 4.3%. Wage growth also increased to 0.4% month over month and 3.7% year over year. However, around 898,000 jobs were erased from payroll estimates between April 2024 and March 2025 due to benchmark revisions. The total nonfarm employment growth for 2025 was reduced from 584,000 to 181,000, with an average of just 15,000 jobs added each month last year. This signifies the weakest year for job creation outside a recession since 2003, sparking a debate among economists on whether January's report indicates stabilization or if it's just statistical noise. Former Fed economist Claudia Sahm highlighted the significant downward revisions, noting that there were more than a million fewer jobs than initially reported. This story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Entera Bio appoints Geno J. Germano as Chairman with Pfizer experience, advancing programs towards 2026 milestones.
data.description: Entera welcomes a new chairman to their board, bringing extensive global leadership experience from prominent biopharma companies like Pfizer and Wyeth. The new addition signals Entera's progression as they work towards achieving significant milestones in 2026 with multiple oral peptide programs. This development marks a pivotal moment for Entera as they strive to drive innovation and growth in the biopharmaceutical industry, leveraging the expertise and insights of their new chairman to propel their initiatives forward.

data.title: Global memory crunch is intensifying, leading to a pricing environment favoring earnings and margins for memory chip players. Goldman Sachs predicts a severe undersupply of DRAM and NAND by roughly 4.9% and 4.2%, respectively, for 2026-27. Server memory drives over 50% of total DRAM demand due to AI servers and data centers, while PC and smartphone growth stalls. Enterprise storage demand is on the rise.
data.description: The global memory market is facing a tightening supply crunch, which is expected to drive prices up and potentially boost earnings and margins for companies in the sector. According to Goldman Sachs analyst Giuni Lee, memory chips like DRAM, NAND, and HBM are experiencing one of the tightest supply/demand landscapes in over a decade. Goldman Sachs predicts a 2026-27 DRAM undersupply of around 4.9% and 2.5% respectively, with a NAND undersupply of about 4.2% and 2.1%, the most severe in over 15 years. Server memory, fueled by AI servers and data centers, is projected to account for more than 50% of total DRAM demand in 2026 and 2027. Meanwhile, demand growth for personal computers and smartphones is slowing due to higher component costs, while enterprise storage demand is on the rise. This trend suggests a prosperous period ahead for memory chip manufacturers.

data.title: Safran forecasts 10% growth for next two years. Positive outlook for 2026. GE Aerospace partner sees rise in shares.
data.description: Safran, partner of GE Aerospace, has increased its projections for the years 2026 and beyond, expecting a 10% expansion in the upcoming two years. This positive development has resulted in a rise in Safran's stock value, signaling promising prospects for the company moving forward.

data.title: Stock market sees declines as Nasdaq and S&P 500 fall below 50-day lines amid AI disruption fears. Cisco, AppLovin, Arista, Vertiv monitored.
data.description: The stock market, which remains divided, began the week on a positive note, but faced setbacks as the Nasdaq and S&P 500 dipped below their respective 50-day moving averages due to concerns over AI disruption. This decline in key levels prompted a focus on prominent companies such as Cisco, AppLovin, Arista, and Vertiv. This weekly review showcased the impact of AI disruption fears on the market's performance.

data.title: High-volume earnings reports this week include RIVN, MNDY, PGY, ON, AMKR, SPOT, CVS, KO, OSCR, DDOG, RACE, SPGI, FI, BP, MAR, DUK, JMIA, HOOD.
data.description: This week will see a high volume of earnings reports from tech, consumer discretionary, and energy sectors, acting as indicators of market momentum and investor sentiment. Notable reports include Monday.com Ltd. (MNDY) and Pagaya Technologies Ltd. (PGY) before market open on Monday, and ON Semiconductor Corp. (ON) and Amkor Technology, Inc. (AMKR) after market close. data.title: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing stock at record high, analyst predicts further rise.
data.description: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) stock has reached a new all-time high in trading, prompting a Wall Street analyst to predict further potential for growth. According to a recent report from Investor's Business Daily, TSM has received a fresh buy rating as it continues to climb. This positive momentum reflects investor confidence in the company's performance.

data.title: Goldman Sachs sees buying opportunities in 4 beaten-down software stocks after sector crash, with IGV falling 22% in 4 weeks.
data.description: Software stocks recently experienced their worst relative selloff in history, with the iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (NYSE: IGV) falling approximately 22% over four weeks — the most significant decline since the pandemic. Compared to the S&P 500 ETF (NYSE: SPY), software underperformed by 21%, the most severe deviation on record, surpassing previous periods like the dot-com bust and the Global Financial Crisis. Goldman Sachs analyst Matthew Martino believes the market panic is due to a rapid shift in investor sentiment rather than deteriorating fundamentals. This fear stems from concerns about artificial intelligence disruption, making four undervalued software stocks appealing investment opportunities according to Goldman Sachs. Companies like Claude Cowork and OpenAI's Frontier are contributing to this narrative, causing multiples to shrink. Despite this, some companies still possess strong data, infrastructure, and essential roles.

data.title: Michael Saylor plans a 12th consecutive Bitcoin buy despite market turmoil, showing Strategy's commitment to accumulating BTC.
data.description: Strategy's consistent accumulation of BTC will reach its twelfth week with the latest purchase, even amidst a significant drop in the company's stock value.

data.title: Romantic film 'Wuthering Heights' nears $82M in global box office with Warner Bros.' $80M budget.
data.description: Warner Bros. invested around $80 million in the production of the R-rated romance film helmed by Emerald Fennell, excluding significant marketing expenses.

data.title: BlackRock ups BMNR holding by 165%, Ark Invest invests $4.2M, BMNR's stock rises 1.5%. Ownership now 9M shares, valued at $245.7M as of Dec 31, 2025, despite losses.
data.description: BitMine Immersion Technologies (NYSE: BMNR) saw a 1.5% increase as BlackRock (NYSE: BLK) revealed a stake increase of 165.6%, along with Cathie Wood's Ark Invest purchasing $4.2 million worth of BMNR shares. BlackRock's 13F-HR filing showed ownership surged from 3.4 million shares to 9 million shares by December 31, 2025, valued at $245.7 million. Despite BitMine's significant unrealized losses from Ethereum's decline, BlackRock raised its stake. Ark Invest also acquired 212,314 shares across three ETFs on Thursday to boost crypto exposure as BMNR trades 36.7% lower in the last month. Additional details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Trump's clash with Powell raises concerns about White House influence on central bank.
data.description: The recent criticisms directed at Powell by the administration are raising concerns that the White House may be attempting to exert too much control over the central bank.

data.title: Perimeter Solutions prices $550 million offering of 6.250% senior secured notes due 2034, to close on Jan. 2, 2026.
data.description: Perimeter Solutions, Inc. announced that its subsidiary, Perimeter Holdings, LLC, priced an offering of $550 million aggregate principal amount of 6.250% senior secured notes due 2034. The Notes will bear interest at 6.250% per year, payable semi-annually, and will mature on January 15, 2034. They will be fully guaranteed on a senior secured basis by Perimeter Intermediate, LLC, and all existing or future restricted subsidiaries. The Notes will be secured by a first-priority security interest in all present and future assets of Perimeter Holdings and guarantors. The offering is expected to close on January 2, 2026.

data.description: The impact of the recent conflict between Trump and Powell is expected to have long-term effects beyond a singular trading day.
